skill.name: image-to-code
skill.description: Generates website design images first, then analyzes them and implements matching frontend code. For visually important web tasks in Codex.
skill.summary: Image-first web design: generate, analyze, then code matching frontend from section images.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: All 101 static findings evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The SKILL.md file is a design instruction document for AI agents, containing UX guidelines, design configuration parameters, and workflow instructions. No shell commands, weak cryptography, or reconnaissance was found after semantic review. The backtick patterns flagged as external_commands are markdown code formatting for design parameter ranges. The crypto and reconnaissance flags are pattern-matching false positives on design/UX terminology.

content.user_title: Turn design ideas into matching website code
content.value_statement: Most AI web design tools produce generic, repetitive layouts that are hard to translate into real code. This skill generates premium design images first, analyzes them deeply, then produces frontend code that faithfully matches the visual reference.
actual_capabilities: Generates section-by-section design images as visual references before writing code
Analyzes generated images for typography, spacing, colors, and layout relationships
Produces frontend code that matches the generated design closely
Supports multi-section landing pages from hero to footer
Enforces design quality rules like clean heroes, readable text, and balanced spacing
Varies section rhythm and layout to avoid repetitive AI-generated patterns
limitations: Requires an AI tool with image generation capabilities for the full workflow
Focused on visually driven websites, not technical web applications or dashboards
Cannot guarantee pixel-perfect output from image analysis to code
Image generation quality depends on the capabilities of the underlying model

use_cases.title: Startup landing page design
use_cases.description: A founder needs a polished multi-section landing page for a new product. The skill generates hero, features, testimonials, and CTA section images, then implements matching frontend code.

use_cases.title: Portfolio website redesign
use_cases.description: A designer wants a premium portfolio site with strong visual identity. The skill creates art-directed section images and produces code that preserves the design intent.

use_cases.title: Marketing site for agencies
use_cases.description: An agency needs a high-end marketing website for a client. The skill generates section images with varied rhythm and layout, then builds frontend code that matches the approved design direction.

prompt_templates.title: Single hero section
prompt_templates.prompt: Design and implement a hero section for a productivity app called FlowSpace. Generate the hero image first, analyze it, then write the frontend code.

prompt_templates.title: Four-section landing page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 4-section landing page for a sustainable fashion brand. Generate section images for hero, features, testimonials, and CTA. Analyze each image and implement the full page.

prompt_templates.title: Eight-section marketing site
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an 8-section marketing site for a SaaS analytics platform. Generate one image per section, extract the design system from all images, and implement the complete frontend.

prompt_templates.title: Creative agency portfolio
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a premium creative agency website with 6 sections. Use strong typography, varied section rhythm, and signature components. Generate separate section images and implement matching code.

output_examples.input: Design a clean hero section for a meditation app called Calm Space.
output_examples.output: The skill first generates a hero section image with the headline, subheadline, and CTA button clearly visible. It analyzes spacing, typography hierarchy, and color palette. Then it produces responsive HTML/CSS code with matching layout, centered headline, soft background gradient, and rounded CTA button.

output_examples.input: Create a 4-section landing page for a coffee subscription service.
output_examples.output: The skill generates 4 separate section images: hero with headline and signup CTA, features section with three benefit cards, testimonial strip with customer quotes, and closing CTA. Each image is analyzed for text, spacing, and colors. The final output is a complete landing page with matching typography, color palette, and section layout.

best_practices: Start each project by describing the visual style you want, including mood, color preferences, and content tone.
Review all generated section images before coding to ensure the design direction matches your expectations.
Specify the number of sections clearly to get the right number of separate images and complete coverage.
anti_patterns: Requesting code directly without image generation first defeats the image-to-code workflow and produces generic output.
Requesting too many sections in a single message can overload the image generation step and reduce quality.
Using vague descriptions like make it look nice without specifying audience or brand context leads to misaligned designs.

faq.question: Does this skill work without image generation?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for an image-first workflow. Without image generation, it will fall back to describing designs rather than generating them, which reduces quality.

faq.question: What kind of websites can this skill create?
faq.answer: It works best for visually driven websites like landing pages, marketing sites, portfolio sites, and product pages. It is not suited for dashboards or web applications.

faq.question: How many sections can it handle at once?
faq.answer: The skill can handle 1 to 12 sections. For best results, request one image per section so text stays readable and design details are extractable.

faq.question: Can I customize the generated design?
faq.answer: Yes. You can describe your preferred style, color palette, and brand context. The skill adapts its design parameters to match your direction.

faq.question: Does it support responsive design?
faq.answer: The skill emphasizes clean spacing and readable layouts that work on small laptops. Exact responsive behavior depends on the frontend implementation.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill contains only design instructions and UX guidelines. It does not execute commands, access files, or make network requests.
